message:
fix(fr-store): make APPEND/SETRANGE promote string encoding to raw [br-frankenredis-84bv]

Upstream Redis 7.2 encodes short strings (<=44 bytes) as 'embstr' but
irreversibly promotes them to 'raw' on the first in-place mutation
(APPEND, SETRANGE, GETSET copy-overwrite, etc.).  Once promoted, the
encoding is sticky -- even if a subsequent operation shrinks the value
back below the embstr threshold, OBJECT ENCODING continues to report
'raw'.  See redis/src/object.c (createStringObject vs
createRawStringObject) and the SDS encoding-promotion paths in t_string.c.

Before this change fr-store derived OBJECT ENCODING purely from the
*current* length of the byte buffer, so a short value that had been
APPEND-ed/SETRANGE-d still reported as 'embstr', diverging from a
vendored redis 7.2.4 oracle on differential probes (port 16700 vs
16701).

Changes:
  - Entry: add a per-entry `force_raw_encoding: bool` flag (default
    false) that mirrors upstream's sticky promotion bit.
  - Store::append: set `force_raw_encoding = true` after extending the
    string in place.
  - Store::setrange: set `force_raw_encoding = true` on both the
    overwrite path (existing string) and the create-new-buffer path
    (key did not previously exist).
  - object_encoding(): short-circuit to "raw" whenever
    `force_raw_encoding` is set, bypassing both the i64-canonical
    "int" branch and the <=44-byte "embstr" branch.

Acceptance (per upstream parity probe):
  SET k short                     -> +OK
  APPEND k _more                  -> :11
  OBJECT ENCODING k               -> "raw"   (was "embstr")
  SET k longer-but-still-short    -> +OK
  SETRANGE k 0 X                  -> :<len>
  OBJECT ENCODING k               -> "raw"   (was "embstr")

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
